_doc: COMMON TRUNK — the domain-agnostic spine every job-based investigation runs FIRST, before any per-domain subtree. Resolve identity -> get the failing entity -> get its logs/exception -> extract the signature -> TOP-LEVEL dispatch on the exception package -> per-domain dispatch. This is the shared prefix; per-domain subtrees should START at their first branch (post-extract), not re-gather. The same ambiguous prompt reaches IS/Excel/UIA/Maestro here, routed by the ACTUAL exception found, not by the prompt.

nodes._mode: llm/guided — identity resolution is the judgment step (per the system guide's Data Correlation: pin folder, then job; ASK the user if ambiguous, never enumerate). Not a deterministic branch.
nodes.cmd: <per system guide: resolve folder, then the failing job/instance>
uip or jobs logs {{job_key}} --level Error --output json

nodes._doc: TOP-LEVEL cross-product router. Routes on the exception FQN package (structured token = exact) to the per-domain dispatch. Stuck ProcessOrchestration parent (no leaf-level exception) routes to the maestro cross-system traversal.

children.judgment_q: Is this a stuck/Running ProcessOrchestration job with NO leaf-level exception (a Maestro-orchestrated parent)? If so it's a cross-system symptom — traverse to the Maestro instance + child fault.

nodes.reason: Could not resolve the failing entity deterministically — LLM resolves identity (asking the user if needed), then re-enters the trunk at get_entity.

Exception package maps to a domain with no built dispatch/subtree yet (Excel/UIA/etc.). Hand to the LLM hypothesis loop seeded with the trunk's gathered signature + the matched playbook from references/exception-table.md.
